docs.title: Welcome!
docs.text:
Welcome to the Stripe for Salesforce app end user documentation!

This documentation aims to guide you through the user actions of our integration application between Stripe and Salesforce.

Whether you're new to Stripe or Salesforce, this documentation will provide you with the information you need to make the most of this application and without having to pivot between the two systems.

docs.title: Introduction
docs.text:
The Stripe for Salesforce app allows you to seamlessly connect your Salesforce org with your Stripe account(s). Enabling you to synchronise data between Stripe and Salesforce in real time.

In the Stripe for Salesforce app you will have the ability to:

  • Automate processes and workflows
  • Send customised invoices to your customers
  • Comprehensively oversee and manage all your products and prices
  • Refund customers directly within Salesforce
  • Understand how and why payments to your Stripe account may fail
  • Sync and manage all your customer records and payment methods
  • Use Salesforce as a virtual terminal to take payments from your customers

In this end user documentation, you will find easy to follow user action guides to help you get started with the Stripe for Salesforce.

Let's dive in and explore the features and functionalities that this integration offers!

docs.text:

docs.title: Charges
docs.text:
The charge object represents a single attempt to move money into your Stripe account. A PaymentIntent confirmation is the most common way to create Charges. The Charge object includes information relating to the amount, currency, customer, and payment method.

Using Stripe for Salesforce app to sync your charges from Stripe to Salesforce, keeps track of all charges, transactions and statuses inside your Salesforce org.

docs.title: Charges in the Stripe for Salesforce app
docs.text:
Charges are read-only records that are provided to the user to highlight every individual transaction.

You can find the Charges located under the Charge object in the Stripe for Salesforce application. The Charge's list view, provides information such as the SCR-xxxxxx (Stripe Charge record name), the amount, the amount refunded, the transaction description, the status and the charge creation date and time.

Clicking on the name of one of the charge opens up the record for further inspection.

In the record we see the similar information as the list view but also information about the transaction amounts, any further Stripe IDs for reference, and any relevant records of refunds.

docs.title: Sync Charges from Stripe
docs.text: If you need to manually sync your payment intents from Stripe, without waiting for the webhooks to automatically complete that, you will need to navigate to the charge record and locate and click the action button Charge. This will pull the data from Stripe to your Salesforce org. testtesttest

docs.title: Charges Statuses
docs.text:
In the table below you will see a list of statuses used by the Stripe for Salesforce application and Stripe regarding charges, as well as a description of the status.

Charge Status Description Succeeded

A charge status of succeeded occurs when a transaction instance has added funds to your account.Or refunds, once the refund has been processed and left your account and reached the customer's payment instrument.

Pending A charge status of pending occurs when there is a delay to the capturing of funds or releasing the funds for refunds. Failed A charge of status of failed occurs when the transaction has failed, been cancelled or interrupted.

docs.title: Customer accounts
docs.text: A customer record in the Stripe for Salesforce app represents your customers. This record can be used to create, update, edit and track customer information.

docs.title: Create a new customer record in the Stripe app and send data
docs.text:
Here we will discuss how to add a new customer record and send the record information from Salesforce to Stripe.

To create a New customer record in the Stripe for Salesforce app. First go to the Account tab and find the New action button, select the Person Account record type you have configured. Then fill in the information form fields and click save.

New Person Account record form fields

Your page will refresh with the newly created customer record, where you can see all the information you previously added. The Stripe for Salesforce application will then sync with Stripe to create and send the customer details.

New customer record

docs.title: Sync patient data to Stripe
docs.text:
Here we will discuss how to send an existing customer record from Salesforce to Stripe.

This is normally done automatically through the integration, but if you need to do this manually please follow these instructions.

To send this customer information to Stripe, click on the Send Customer action button. And, if you want to pull any data manually from Stripe, click on the Sync Stripe. Once the request has been sent to Stripe or from Stripe, you will then be shown a completion message once the flow is complete.

Customer record > Send customer data to Stripe

Customer record > Sync customer data from Stripe

Customer record > send data to stripe completion message

docs.title: Verify that patients are available in Stripe.
docs.text:
Here we will discuss how to verify the customer record has synced correctly with Stripe.

Go to your customers area on Stripe. Next, search for the customer you need.

Stripe Dashboard > Customers

Go to the left hand side of the window and view the details dropdown. Here you should see a customer ID number and this will match the record you see in Salesforce.

Stripe Dashboard > Customers > Details: Stripe ID

Customer record > Stripe ID

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
This document aims to provide users with goal-orientated information for actions within an implemented version of the Stripe for Salesforce app.

We provide you guided steps to use Stripe for Salesforce to the best of its ability and with the greatest of ease. For example, in the Customer accounts page we will walk you through adding new contacts Salesforce and syncing these to Stripe, or in Refunds you will be shown how and where to generate a refund to your customer.

For further information on using the Stripe for Salesforce app, any in-depth training questions, issues or installation requirements you can contact the team at support@gazela.io .

docs.title: User Interface
docs.text:
Once the Stripe for Salesforce app is correctly implemented on your Salesforce organisation, you will be able to see all the tabs and objects related to Stripe.

To view these, first we need to launch the Stripe for Salesforce app. Click on the to open the app manager and search for the Stripe for Salesforce app.

Once you load the Stripe for Salesforce app, you will see that the app includes the following Standard Salesforce objects; Accounts and Products. As well as custom Stripe objects; such as payment methods, invoices, subscriptions, payment intents, charges, Stripe accounts and Stripe Webhook events. All of which are Stripe event objects used to sync actions between the two systems.

docs.title: Feedback and contact
docs.text:
Our end user documentation lays out guided steps on how to maximise the use of and guarantee user success when using the Stripe for Salesforce app.

We welcome any feedback on our end user documentation and/or if you have any questions, queries or issues with the Stripe for Salesforce app please do not hesitate to contact us.

You can contact our support at support@gazela.io

Thank you.

docs.title: How it works
docs.text: The Stripe for Salesforce app will automatically synchronise the information between Salesforce and Stripe.

docs.title: Data Model
docs.text:
The Stripe for Salesforce app contains a data model that mirrors the data store inside Stripe.

This information that is synchronised:

  • Products
  • Pricing
  • Customers
  • Invoices
  • Charges
  • Payment Methods
  • Subscriptions

docs.title: Automatically Updating Stripe
docs.text: When a record is created or updated in Salesforce, the Stripe for Salesforce app will automatically create or update Stripe, using existing prebuilt Salesforce Flow automations that are included in the Stripe for Salesforce app.

docs.title: Automatically Updating Salesforce
docs.text: When a record is created or updated in Stripe, the Stripe for Salesforce app will automatically create or update Salesforce, because it will receive a webhook message from Stripe which it will process.

docs.title: Invoices
docs.text: In the Stripe for Salesforce app, invoices can be sent to customers directly from a Salesforce org. Users are able to generate and send new invoices to customers, alongside update record the updates to the invoice status or stages within Salesforce. The updates to this workflow will then also be synced with Salesforce.

docs.title: Invoice generation
docs.text:
To generate a new invoice for a customer, the user must first navigate to the Customer Account. From here the user will need to locate and click the Raise Invoice action button on the page layout.

Next fill in the form fields with the information required for this invoice. Start by adding a description and due date.

On the next window select the product.

And, finally add the price and quantities for the invoice. Here the user also can choose to add more items to the invoice by click the box for Create More Items, this will then take the user back to the first model window to input their invoice line item information.

Once this is is completed, the user can navigate to the invoice record and see the invoice in its draft form. This can be edited and revised before being sent to the customer and Stripe.

docs.title: Send the invoice to your customer
docs.text:
Once the user has generated an invoice and is ready to send it to the customer they will need to: navigate to the invoice record and select the Send To Customer action button on the page layout. Clicking the Send To Customer initiates the process in which Salesforce tells Stripe to send the invoice to the customer.

Upon completing this action the status of the draft invoice is then changed to Open. As you'll see here the invoice status is reflected within both Salesforce and the Stripe dashboard.

The customer will then receive an invoice with a payment link to a hosted payment page, where payment information can be entered.

Emailed invoice and payment link example:

Hosted payment page example:

docs.title: Manual syncing invoice data from Stripe
docs.text:
Here we will discuss how to sync an invoice record from Salesforce to Stripe. This is normally done through the integrated webhooks, but if you need to do this manually please follow these instructions.

To send this invoice to Stripe, click on the sync invoice action button in the Product record. You will then be shown a completion message.

Then if you check your Stripe dashboard in the Invoices section you will see and updated list of Invoices and invoice payment statuses.

docs.title: Payment intents
docs.text:
A PaymentIntent serves as a roadmap for securing a payment from your customer. Throughout its journey, a PaymentIntent progresses through various stages while interacting with Stripe.js to conduct authentication procedures, culminating in the creation of a single successful transaction at most.

Info

NOTE: If you want to take payments from Salesforce, you need to use it with Stripe.Js to be PCI compliant.

docs.title: Payment intents in Stripe app for salesforce
docs.text:
PaymentIntents are read-only records that are provided to the user to highlight the status of each individual transaction.

You can find the Stripe for Salesforce PaymentIntents located under the Payment Intents object in the Stripe for Salesforce application. In the list view will provide information such as the PI-xxxxxx (payment intent record name), the transaction description, the PaymentIntent status and the Stripe Payment Intent ID.

Clicking on the name of one of the Payment Intent opens up the record for further inspection. In the record we see the similar information as the list view but also information about the transaction amounts, any further Stripe IDs for reference, the charge that was made to the customer's payment method, alongside any refunds and Payment Intent History.

docs.title: Verifying the PaymentIntent status with Stripe
docs.text:
To verify the status of the transactions in the Stripe for Salesforce application you can go to your Stripe Dashboard > payments.

As you can see here the status of the payment is recorded on both the list view in the Stripe dashboard and in the individual timeline of each stripe payment record (below).

docs.title: Sync Intent from Stripe
docs.text: If you need to manually sync your payment intents from Stripe, without waiting for the webhooks to automatically complete that, you will need to navigate to the paymentintent record and locate and click the action button Sync Intent. This will pull the data from Stripe to your Salesforce org.

docs.title: PaymentIntents Statuses
docs.text:
In the table below you will see a list of statuses used by the Stripe for Salesforce application and Stripe regarding payment intents, as well as a description of the status.

PaymentIntent Status Description Requires_payment_method

A PaymentIntent with the status requires/_payment/_method indicates that the payment flow has been created and requires a payment method to be attached.If the payment attempt fails (for example due to a decline), the PaymentIntent’s status returns to requires/_payment/_method so that the payment can be retried.

Requires_confirmation

A PaymentIntent with the status of requires/_confirmation occurs once the customer has entered payment information. This indicates that the PaymentIntent is ready to be confirmed.

Requires_action If a PaymentIntent has the requires_action status this indicates that the payment requires additional authentication with 3D Secure. Requires-capture

A PaymentIntent with the status of requires/_capture instructs Stripe to authorise the amount from the payment method but not to capture the funds. Authorising a payment guarantees the amount by holding it on the customer’s payment method. If successful this moves the PaymentIntent status to processing.Example: hotels often authorise a payment in full before a guest arrives, then capture the money when the guest checks out.

Processing

Once all required actions are handled, the PaymentIntent status will be moved to processing . Processing times may vary depending on payment method.

Succeeded

A PaymentIntent that has the status succeeded refers to the payment flow being complete.

Funds will now have left the customer's payment method and will now be in your account.

Cancelled

The cancelled status can occur at any stage before processing or succeeded. If a PaymentIntent has the status cancelled becomes invalidated and cannot be used for future payment attempts. Any funds being held i.e., status: requires/_capture are released when the status updates to cancelled.

docs.title: Payment methods
docs.text:
In Stripe payment methods are how customers pay for goods or services with Stripe. Stripe supports a range of different payment methods. In the Stripe App for Salesforce, Payment Methods are mostly read-only data related to Accounts.

When a customer enters their payment information i.e., card number, expiry date and billing information, this is stored in Stripe. The payment method is a record synced from Stripe and held against the account record, alongside other transactional data.

docs.title: Payment Method record
docs.text:
In Stripe for Salesforce, we create a record of the customer payment method and any changes to the information that the customer has entered during the payment process, for example, if a customer has changed their card when next paying an invoice or adds a new billing address to their next payment and this is overwritten in the record and captured.

You can find your customer's payment method records by navigating to the Payment Method object.

As you can see from the example provided the Payment Method record provides an oversight to transactional data such as payment method type, card brand, the last 4 digits of the payment card.

Other related information such as PaymentIntents and Charges made with this payment method can be viewed from the Payment method record. These records are intrinsically linked to the Customer Accounts, PaymentIntents and charges to capture accurate transactional data.

docs.title: Prices
docs.text:
The Stripe price object is like a product's price tag. It includes info like the price's amount, currency, recurring billing info, and usage type. There are two types of prices in Stripe: one-time prices and recurring prices. Price data in Stripe is mapped to a custom object in Salesforce.

Info

Note: The Stripe for Salesforce app only lets you add one-time prices from Salesforce. If you want to add recurring prices, you'll need to do it in Stripe and then sync it back to Salesforce. Tier-based pricing isn't supported in the current Stripe for Salesforce app.

docs.title: Create a price from Salesforce:
docs.text:
To create a price for a Product in the Stripe app for Salesforce, firstly go to the Product object and select a Product record.

Product record > Create price action button

Next we go to the create price action button on the record layout. Next fill in the form fields with the information required and click save. This sends and syncs the information to Stripe.

We can then view the related tab on the product record to see the prices attributed to this product record.

Product record > related > Stripe prices for the product record

docs.title: Manual Syncing Products From Stripe
docs.text:
Here we will discuss how to sync a product's price record from Salesforce to Stripe. This is normally done through the integrated webhooks, but if you need to do this manually please follow these instructions.

To send this product information to Stripe, click on the Sync Pice action button in the Product record page layout. If you go to the product catalogue of the Stripe dashboard, you'll be able to see that your product has an associated price. Entering the product catalogue record in Stripe will give you further information about when the price was added to the product.

docs.title: Products
docs.text: A product object in Stripe represents a physical or digital good or service that you sell. It can be used to create invoices, charge subscriptions, and track inventory.

docs.title: Create new product in Stripe app
docs.text:
Here we will look at how to create a new product in the Stripe app.

Firstly, we need to go to the Products tab and click on the new action button on the page layout.

Product tab > New action button

Then fill in the product information form fields and click save (or save and new). Locate the new product record, and you are able see all the information about the product you just added.

New product form fields

New Product

New product details

docs.title: Edit products in stripe app for salesforce
docs.text:
Now let’s take a look at how to edit a product in the Stripe app for Salesforce. Here we will be able to edit a number of fields on the Product record and sync with Stripe.

Go to the product tab and locate the product record that is being edited.

Product tab > Product record to edit

Next we navigate to and click the (downward arrow) action button on the page layout and select edit.

Product tab > Edit

Product record > Edit

Now we can edit the fields that we need to and click save. Locate the product record, and you are able see all the information about the product you just edited.

Product tab > Edit the product fields

Product record > Edit the product fields

docs.title: Manual Syncing Products From Stripe
docs.text:
Here we will discuss how to sync a product record from Salesforce to Stripe. This is normally done through the integrated webhooks, but if you need to do this manually please follow these instructions.

To send this product information to Stripe, click on the Send Product action button in the Product record. Or to Sync products from Stripe to Salesforce click Sync Product. Once the request has been sent to Stripe or from Stripe, you will then be shown a completion message once the flow is complete.

Sync flow completion message

To verify whether the product is synced with Stripe, you can go to your Stripe account and look at the product catalogue and view the synced products.

docs.title: Refunds
docs.text: In the Stripe for Salesforce app, refunds can be sent to customers directly from a Salesforce org. Users are able to generate and send refunds to customers, alongside update the payment intents and charge records. The updates to this workflow will then also be synced with Stripe.

docs.title: Refund generation
docs.text:
To generate a refund to a customer, the user must first navigate to the Payment intent tab. From here the user will need to select a payment intent record from which the refund will be sent.

Next locate and click the Refund action button on the page layout.

Clicking the refund action button opens a modal window in Salesforce. The amount is populated from the succeeded payment from the customer - this is editable by clicking into the form field and changing the numerical amount.

Next we need to select a reason for the refund, this is done by clicking into the reason form field. One the reason has been confirmed, click next and the refund will be processed.

Coming back to the payment intent record, we can see the refund is succeeded.

docs.title: Verifying Refunds in the Stripe Dashboard
docs.text:
To verify the status of the refund in the Stripe for Salesforce application you can go to your Stripe Dashboard > payments.

As you can see here the status of the refund is recorded on both the list view in the Stripe dashboard and in the individual timeline of each Stripe payment record (below).

docs.title: Stripe webhook events
docs.text:
Webhooks are automated messages sent from applications when something happens. They're a way for different software systems, in our case Stripe and Salesforce, to communicate with each other in real-time. Essentially, a webhook sends a signal to another system or server over the web, which typically carries with it a payload of data.

In Stripe for Salesforce, the API calls are made automatically; we can view the log of all webhooks as they sync and send data between Stripe and Salesforce. If there is ever a problem, or you don't think a data in being transferred between the two systems, this is where you can view the data exchanges.

docs.title: Stripe Webhooks Events In Stripe for Salesforce
docs.text:
The webhook events are read-only records that are provided to the user to highlight the status of each exchange of data between Stripe and Salesforce.

You can find the Webhook events located under the Stripe Webhook events object in the Stripe for Salesforce application. In the webhook events list view will provide information such as the SWE-xxxxxx (Stripe webhook event record name), the event type (the Stripe event that is called), the webhook status,the Stripe object ID, and creation and modification dates.

Clicking on the name of one of the Stripe webhook events opens up the record for further inspection. In the record we see the similar information as the list view but also information about the statuses of the webhooks and any webhook failure information.

docs.title: Stripe Webhook event Statuses
docs.text:
In the table below you will see a list of statuses used by the Stripe for Salesforce application and Stripe regarding Webhook events, as well as a description of the status.

Webhook events statuses Description Success A webhook event status of success occurs when a an API call is made (either automatically or manually) and it returns with a data payload or a status change etc. Failed A webhook event status of failed occurs when a an API call is made (either automatically or manually) and it does not return with the data payload requested or a status change etc.

docs.title: Subscriptions
docs.text:
Stripe subscriptions are a way to bill your customers on a regular basis. This is a great way to charge for products or services that are delivered over time.

The Stripe for Salesforce app can be used to add subscriptions for a customer directly from within Salesforce, so you don't have to switch to Stripe. It can also sync changes from Stripe.

docs.title: Create and set up subscriptions from the Stripe app for Salesforce.
docs.text:
To create a subscription in Salesforce, go to the Customer Account tab and click on the Create Subscription button in the page layout.

From here the user will need to fill out the modal window fields. Selecting the product(s), the cost(s) and quantities to add to the subscription.

If you don't see the product or price you may need to create or sync a new product and/or price record.

Once completed, the subscription record will be updated with the related subscription items, i.e. products and prices.

Navigating to and clicking on the Subscription item record, will display the relevant subscription item information.

docs.title: Manual sync subscription to stripe
docs.text: Entering the subscription record, the user is able to manually sync the subscription record to Stripe by clicking on the Send customer action button. Completing this step will produce a completion message and you will see a status change to active on the Subscription record.

docs.title: Verifying the subscription in Stripe
docs.text: B.y logging into the Stripe dashboard and navigating to the developer area and clicking on Subscriptions on the left hand column. The user can the review the synced subscriptions statuses from this area in the Stripe dashboard.

docs.title: What is a virtual terminal?
docs.text: A virtual terminal is an online application that allows users to input and process card payments using an online application, bypassing the need for physical card-reading hardware.

docs.title: Using Salesforce as a virtual terminal
docs.text:
The Stripe for Salesforce app is designed to empower users to leverage the payment processing capabilities of Stripe, transforming Salesforce into a virtual terminal for processing payments.

The Stripe for Salesforce integration enables users to collect payment information from customers through the telephone, through emailed invoices, and/or payment links.
